{"step_by_step_thinking": "Whiplash injury is a type of neck injury that can occur in a car accident, but it does not provide a definitive indication of who was the driver. Steering wheel imprint would suggest the person who was holding the steering wheel at the time of the accident, which would likely be the driver. Sparrow foot marks could indicate the person who stepped on the brake or accelerator, which might also be the driver. Seat belt abrasion over the left shoulder could indicate the person who was wearing the seat belt, which would likely be the driver. Therefore, whiplash injury is the feature that does not specifically indicate who was the driver.", "answer_choice": "A"}
